name: GitHub
one_liner: GitHub is the leading developer platform for hosting Git repositories, code collaboration, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD, all exposed through a comprehensive public API.
access_notes: Anyone can create a free GitHub account and immediately generate a fine-grained or classic personal access token, or register an OAuth App / GitHub App, to get working API credentials. No payment or human approval required for standard REST/GraphQL API access.
api_notes: Extensive REST API (docs.github.com/rest) covering repos, issues, pull requests, actions, packages, orgs, users, gists, checks, security, and more, plus a full GraphQL API (v4). Hundreds of endpoints. Tokens sent via Authorization: Bearer header; username/password auth not supported.
mcp_notes: GitHub ships an official MCP server (github/github-mcp-server). Available as a hosted remote server at https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ (OAuth or PAT) and as a self-hostable local Docker image (ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server) supporting GitHub Enterprise Server. Covers repos, issues, PRs, Actions, code analysis.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and broad public API. Rate limits and GitHub App installation flows are the main operational considerations.

evidence.claim: GitHub REST API supports fine-grained/classic personal access tokens, GitHub Apps, OAuth Apps, and basic auth with client ID/secret; username+password not supported.
GitHub maintains an official MCP server offering repo, issue, PR, and Actions capabilities, available hosted at api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ and self-hosted via Docker.
